title: pedicularis rex subsp. pseudocyathus
intro: Pedicularis rex subsp. pseudocyathus, also known as the Royal Lousewort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bumblebee balm family (Orobanchaceae) that obtains its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pedicularis rex subsp. pseudocyathus include reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
